name: Banda Kopir Tarkari (Vegetables Stir Fried with Spices)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Potatoes, 225 g. chopped into small cubes
recipeInstructions:
In Bangladesh, cabbage is usually available in the market during the winter season, as are tomatoes, peas and carrots. So this dish appears quite frequently at Bengali dinner tables during the winter. In the markets where such vegetables are available year round, banda is a popular standard.
1. Heat oil in a heavy pan and add turmeric and salt. Fry for a few seconds.
2. Add cubed potatoes and stir frequently until they turn yellow from the turmeric. Cook for 5-10 minutes (they will complete their cooking later) and remove them from the oil, setting aside.
3. If necessary, add more oil to the pan. Sauté onion slices until soft and transparent.
4. Add bay leaf, cumin, ginger, and chili powder, stirring well.
5. Put in tomatoes and let them break down.
6. Add cabbage gradually, stirring it in well so that it is sautéed in the spices. Cook gently for 3-5 minutes.
7. Add peas and semi-fried potatoes, mixing well. Replace the cover and continue to cook for 5-10 minutes or until the potatoes are ready.
Serves: 4
